label: Five scoring dimensions defined for skill benchmarking: routing/activation accuracy (0.30), unprompted reference discovery (0.20), efficiency/bottlenecks (0.15), usefulness via skill-on/skill-off ablation (0.25), structural connectivity (0.10) — with concrete observable metrics for each

Skill-advisor routing precision and in-skill smart-router accuracy are distinct sub-dimensions of D1 routing accuracy — the advisor provides gold-standard routing expectations via advisor_recommend confidence thresholds

Normalization must be per-skill (baseline→delta) not cross-skill absolute — skills of different sizes (sk-code 40+ refs vs a 3-intent utility) are not comparable on raw tool-call counts; complexity-relative thresholds and family-relative ranking are needed

BFCL v4s AST-based tool-call evaluation and Irrelevance Detection category directly transfer to Lane C — parse AI tool-call traces as ASTs of Read/Glob calls and validate against expected resource trees per intent

ToolBench/ToolEvals scenario construction pipeline (API collection → instruction generation → solution-path annotation) is the template for Lane C fixture generation — automate from skills own trigger_phrases and INTENT_SIGNALS
Self-RAGs retrieval necessity signal maps to Lane Cs core question: did loading a reference actually help produce the output? — can be tracked via structured trace events after each reference load
Lane C evaluation should be execution-based (trace parsing, file-exists checks, keyword matching) wherever possible; LLM-as-judge reserved for qualitative D4 ablation comparison only

sk-doc validation (DQI, template compliance, frontmatter checks) and manual testing playbooks are structurally distinct from Lane C — sk-doc validates document shape, Lane C validates real-world AI utilization at runtime

The rename surface (Phase 002) covers 8+ touch-point surfaces: skill dir, SKILL.md frontmatter/triggers/keywords, 3 commands, agent file, 4 runtime mirrors, advisor graph, descriptions.json, sentinel sk-prompt-small-model, root CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, internal self-references — rename must execute before Lane C build

The 121 Lane B 19-phase arc (design→research→build→harden→review→remediate→doc) is the proven template for Lane C build — three pluggable seams (candidate-source, dispatcher, scorer) are shared across all lanes
